data.title: Regenxbio Shares Rise on Positive Long-Term Data for Hunter Syndrome Drug
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Regenxbio shares rose in early trading Friday after it revealed positive long-term data for the trials of its Hunter syndrome treatment.

Shares rose 4.7% higher to $9.86.

The biotechnology company announced the new positive data from the Phase I, II and III Campsiite trial of clemidsogene lanparvovec, also known as RGX-121, for the treatment of patients with Mucopolysaccharidosis Type II, also known as Hunter syndrome.

Hunger syndrom is a rare inherited genetic disorder that is part of a group of conditions called lysosomal storage disorders. The disorders, which primarily affect boys, interferes with the body's ability to break down certain complex sugars called glycosaminoglycans.

Regenxbio's 12-month pivotal data showed that a one-time RGX-121 treatment can improve outcomes for patients with the disorder.

The Food and Drug Administration is expected to make a decision on the application by Feb. 8 of next year.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: ABM INDUSTRIES INC /DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
ABM Industries Inc., a leading provider of integrated facility solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter, showcasing robust financial and operational performance. The company experienced significant growth across various segments, driven by organic expansion, strategic acquisitions, and operational efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $2,224.0 million, an increase of $129.8 million or 6.2% compared to the prior year period, driven by organic growth and acquisitions.
  • Operating Profit: $83.4 million, an increase of $46.0 million compared to the prior year period, primarily due to revenue increases across all industry groups and operational efficiencies.
  • Net Income: $41.8 million, an increase of $37.1 million compared to the prior year period, reflecting improved operating performance.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: $0.67, compared to $0.07 in the prior year period, indicating significant improvement in profitability.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.67, compared to $0.07 in the prior year period, reflecting enhanced earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Business & Industry (B&I) segment saw a revenue increase of 2.8% to $1,038.7 million, driven by client expansions and strategic pricing decisions.
    • The Manufacturing & Distribution (M&D) segment experienced an 8.4% increase to $408.9 million, attributed to business expansion and new wins.
    • Aviation revenues rose by 8.7% to $291.8 million due to new business and scope expansions.
    • Education revenues increased by 3.0% to $235.1 million, driven by net new business and scope expansions.
    • Technical Solutions revenues grew by 19.0% to $249.5 million, with organic growth and contributions from recent acquisitions.
  • Operational Efficiencies: Operational efficiencies were noted in the Aviation and Education segments, contributing to improved profit margins. The Education segment, in particular, saw a 109 basis point increase in operating profit margin due to better management of overtime, materials, and administrative costs.
  • Future Outlook: The company has launched a restructuring program in the fourth quarter of 2025 aimed at streamlining operations and improving support function efficiency. This initiative is expected to deliver approximately $35.0 million in annualized cost savings once fully implemented in early 2026.
  • Segment Performance:
    • The Aviation segment's operating profit increased by 11.0% due to contract mix and operational efficiencies.
    • The Education segment's operating profit rose by 17.2%, driven by operational efficiencies.
    • Technical Solutions saw an 8.5% increase in operating profit, although the margin slightly decreased due to service mix and higher amortization of intangible assets.
  • Geographical Performance: The B&I segment's revenue growth was supported by client expansions both domestically and internationally, indicating strong performance across different regions.
  • Management Commentary: Management highlighted strategic pricing decisions and proactive contract extensions as key factors in maintaining and expanding customer accounts, particularly within the B&I and M&D segments.
  • Restructuring Program: The restructuring program initiated in the fourth quarter of 2025 is part of the company's strategy to align its cost structure with strategic growth objectives, aiming for enhanced organizational effectiveness.

SEC Filing: ABM INDUSTRIES INC /DE/ [ ABM ] - 10-Q - Sep. 05, 2025

data.title: Brent Crude Falls to 3-Month Low
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures fell to $65.5 per barrel on Friday, a three-month low, extending the sharp declines from the two prior sessions amid ample supply and uncertain demand.

Reports suggested that OPEC+ will raise oil production quotas ahead of this weekend's meeting, with group leader Saudi Arabia setting the pace for the push.

Such an output increase would coincide with an oil market that is already oversupplied amid high production from non-OPEC+ members and softening demand among the glob's top fuel consumers, as the cartel aims to recover its market share following pandemic-period cuts and multiple nations in the group need to boost government budget revenues.

Despite slowing manufacturing activity, tanker movements showed that Russian oil exports to China surged, taking up lost demand for India following the latter's tariffs from the US. In the meantime, EIA data showed that oil stocks in the US unexpectedly rose by 2.4 million barrels in the last week of August.

data.title: Tri-Continental Corporation Declares Third Quarter Distribution
data.description:
BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 05, 2025--

Tri-Continental Corporation (the "Corporation") today declared a third quarter ordinary income distribution of $0.2654 per share of Common Stock and $0.6250 per share of Preferred Stock. Distributions on Common Stock will be paid on September 24, 2025 to Common Stockholders of record on September 16, 2025, and dividends on Preferred Stock will be paid on October 1, 2025 to Preferred Stockholders of record on September 16, 2025. The ex-dividend date for both the Common Stock and the Preferred Stock is September 16, 2025. The $0.2654 per share ordinary income distribution on the Common Stock is in accordance with the Corporation's distribution policy.

The Corporation has paid dividends on its common stock for 81 consecutive years. The Corporation's investment manager is Columbia Management Investment Advisers, LLC, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Ameriprise Financial, Inc.

The Corporation's distributions on common stock will vary. The Corporation's current distributions (as estimated by the Corporation based on current information) are from the earnings and profits of the Corporation. No amount of the Corporation's current distribution consists of a return of capital (i.e., a return of some or all of your original investment in the Corporation).

The net asset value of the Corporation's common shares may not always correspond to the market price of such shares. Shares of many closed-end funds frequently trade at a discount from their net asset value. An investment in the Corporation is subject to stock market risk, which is the risk that market prices for the Corporation's common shares may decline over short or long periods, adversely affecting the value of an investment in the Corporation.

Securities selected for the Corporation using quantitative methods may perform differently from the market as a whole, and there can be no assurance that this methodology will enable it to achieve its objective. The Corporation's portfolio investments are subject to market risk, which may affect a single issuer, sector of the economy, industry or the market as a whole. Fixed-income investments, including convertible securities, are subject to credit risk, interest rate risk, and prepayment and extension risk. These risks may be more pronounced for longer-term securities and high-yield securities ("junk bonds"). In general, bond prices rise when interest rates fall and vice versa. Convertible securities are subject to both the risks of their security type prior to conversion as well as their security type after conversion. The Corporation's use of leverage, including through its preferred stock, exposes it to greater risks due to unanticipated market movements, which may magnify losses and increase volatility of returns.

data.title: ProScore Launches V2 Platform at RE+ 2025
data.description:
At RE+ 2025, ProScore will showcase how V2 compresses compliance reporting from months to minutes, validates prevailing wage classification on an intuitive-AI basis, and reports apprenticeship ratios across multi-tier contractors with fully integrated dashboards.

AUSTIN, TX / ACCESS Newswire / September 5, 2025 / ProScore will be announcing the launch of Version 2.0 of its compliance management platform at RE+ 2025, taking place September 8-11 in Las Vegas. The new release introduces expanded capabilities for real-time audit readiness, and unified contractor oversight, giving developers and EPCs the transparency required to manage the complexities of the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), reducing total operating cost (TOC) by up to 60 percent.

"ProScore was built for rapid deployment; our AI integration accelerates the support of Utility-Scale Projects substantially reducing ramp-up time," said Ron Nickelson, founder of ProScore. "With V2, projects can anticipate risks, adapt in real time, and prove compliance the moment auditors or investors demand it. That transparency doesn't just reduce risk-it protects billions of dollars in incentives."

At RE+ 2025, ProScore will showcase how V2 compresses compliance reporting from months to minutes, validates prevailing wage classification on an intuitive-AI basis, and reports apprenticeship ratios across multi-tier contractors with fully integrated dashboards. Executives will also meet with developers, investors, and insurers to demonstrate how the platform strengthens project bankability by aligning compliance with ESG performance.

ProScore actively supports projects involving more than 40 percent of the U.S. utility-scale EPC market, including some of the nation's most complex renewable builds. One major developer recently cut its compliance turnaround from three months to just 24 hours, accelerating investor approval and moving a flagship project into construction ahead of schedule.

As energy regulations tighten and audits become routine, ProScore positions compliance as a driver of resilience rather than a bottleneck. From the jobsite to the boardroom, ProScore delivers the transparency and confidence needed to de-risk projects, protect credits, and accelerate clean energy growth.

About ProScore

ProScore is the industry standard for IRA compliance. Powered by NVIDIA GB200 superchips, our platform delivers an industry-low Total Operational Cost, enhances data accuracy, and provides real-time reporting with unmatched speed and transparency. Learn more at https://proscore.ai.

data.title: ICE Canola Rising in Early Trading
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--ICE canola futures were posting solid gains Friday morning, seeing a continuation of Thursday's correction off five-month lows.

China's Ministry of Commerce said Friday it would prolong its anti-dumping probe into Canadian canola for six months, with a final ruling now not expected until March 9, 2026. The extension raised some optimism that a deal could be reached, although preliminary Chinese duties of 75.8% imposed on Canadian canola seed imports in August remain in place. A Canadian trade delegation, including Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe and Prime Minister Mark Carney's parliamentary secretary Kody Blois, is set to travel to China this weekend.

Chicago soybeans and European rapeseed futures were higher, providing some underlying support. However, soyoil was softer and the Malaysian palm oil market was closed for a holiday.

Seasonal harvest pressure amid a lack of significant weather concerns remained a bearish influence in the background.

About 24,100 canola contracts had traded as of 9:48 a.m. EDT.

data.title: CONSUMERS BANCORP INC /OH/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Consumers Bancorp, Inc., a community-oriented financial institution and bank holding company for Consumers National Bank, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the highly regulated banking industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $53.05 million, reflecting an increase due to higher yields on interest-earning assets.
  • Net Interest Income: $34.11 million, up from $31.99 million in the previous year, driven by an increase in the yield on average interest-earning assets.
  • Net Income: $8.67 million, a slight increase from $8.58 million in the prior year, supported by higher net interest income and noninterest income.
  • Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Share: $2.77, consistent with the previous year's $2.76, indicating stable earnings performance.
  • Total Noninterest Income: $5.45 million, an increase of 11.3% from the prior year, primarily due to higher debit card interchange income and bank-owned life insurance income.
  • Total Noninterest Expenses: $28.09 million, an increase of 8.7% from the previous year, mainly due to higher salaries, occupancy, and equipment expenses.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Consumers Bancorp, Inc. is a bank holding company that primarily holds the common stock of Consumers National Bank. The Bank is a community-oriented financial institution offering a range of commercial and consumer loan and deposit products, as well as mortgage, financial planning, and investment services.
  • Market Area: The Bank operates primarily in Carroll, Columbiana, Jefferson, Mahoning, Stark, and Summit counties in Ohio, with additional markets in northeast Ohio, western Pennsylvania, and northern West Virginia.
  • Branch Network: As of June 30, 2025, the Bank had 22 full-service branch locations and one loan production office, indicating a strong physical presence in its market area.
  • Loan Portfolio: The Bank's loan portfolio includes commercial & industrial loans, commercial real estate loans, farmland loans, land development loans, 1-4 family residential real estate loans, and consumer loans. The portfolio saw a 7.2% increase in loan receivables to $813,458 as of June 30, 2025.
  • Consumer Loans Growth: There was a significant increase in consumer loans by 54.5%, attributed to the expansion of indirect auto lending within the Bank's market areas.
  • Non-Performing Loans: Non-performing loans were 0.11% of total loans as of June 30, 2025, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, with management actively monitoring these loans.
  • Allowance for Credit Losses: The allowance for credit losses as a percentage of total loans remained stable at 1.04%, indicating consistent management of credit risk.
  • Deposit Growth: Total deposits increased by 6.6% to $1.04 billion, with a favorable funding mix that includes a significant portion of noninterest-bearing demand deposits.
  • Liquidity Management: The Bank maintains a strong liquidity position with significant sources of funds, including deposits, lines of credit, and access to the Federal Home Loan Bank of Cincinnati.
  • Cybersecurity and Compliance: The Bank is actively engaged in maintaining robust cybersecurity measures and compliance with regulatory requirements, including recent updates from the SEC and federal banking regulators.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the Bank's market areas, with a focus on maintaining strong asset quality and capital adequacy while navigating economic and regulatory challenges.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Consumers Bancorp, Inc. has focused on maintaining a strong liquidity position by increasing total assets to $1.17 billion, primarily through a $54.3 million increase in total loans funded by a $63.8 million increase in total deposits. The company has also expanded its indirect auto lending within its market areas, contributing to a significant increase in consumer loans.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its total shareholders’ equity by $12.6 million, driven by net income and a decrease in accumulated other comprehensive loss. It paid $2.4 million in cash dividends and maintained a dividend payout ratio of 27.5%. The company also issued shares through stock-based incentive plans and a dividend reinvestment plan, contributing to the increase in common shares. Additionally, the company has maintained a favorable funding mix with a significant portion of deposits in noninterest-bearing demand deposits.
  • Future Outlook: Consumers Bancorp, Inc. plans to continue leveraging its strong liquidity position to support growth initiatives, including potential increases in the residential mortgage warehouse line-of-credit as mortgage volumes rise. The company also intends to maintain its focus on community banking, which has resulted in a favorable deposit mix and lower cost of funds. The company expects to manage its capital resources effectively to support ongoing growth and shareholder returns.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory Environment: Consumers Bancorp, Inc. operates in a highly regulated environment, subject to oversight by multiple federal and state regulatory bodies, including the SEC, Federal Reserve Board, and OCC. Changes in laws or regulatory policies could materially impact the company's business and prospects.
  • Economic Risks: Changes in local, regional, and national economic conditions could lead to a deterioration in asset credit quality, impacting debtors' ability to meet obligations due to high unemployment rates and inflationary pressures.
  • Interest Rate Risks: Rapid fluctuations in market interest rates could affect fair market valuations and lead to a decline in net interest income.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes in trade, monetary, and fiscal policies, including interest rate policies of the Federal Reserve Board, could affect the company's operations.
  • Liquidity Risks: Unanticipated changes in liquidity position, including the cost of liquidity and the ability to find alternative funding sources, could pose challenges.
  • Competitive Risks: Increased competitive pressures on product pricing and services could impact the company's market position.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: Breaches of security or failures of technology systems due to technological factors and cybersecurity threats could adversely affect operations.
  • Operational Risks: Changes in consumer spending, borrowing, and savings habits, as well as declining asset values impacting collateral, could affect the company's financial stability.
  • Human Resource Risks: The ability to attract and retain qualified employees is crucial for maintaining operational efficiency.

data.description:
RNS Number : 2726Y Bank of Montreal 05 September 2025  

ANNEX B

Form of RNS Announcement

Bank of Montreal

September 5, 2025

Regulatory Notice

Publication of Final Terms

Bank of Montreal has agreed to issue GBP 500,000,000 Callable Fixed to Floating Rate Senior Notes due October 2031, Series 437, (the "Notes") pursuant to its U.S.$40,000,000,000 Note issuance Programme (the "Programme").

The following document constitutes the final terms dated September 3, 2025 (the "Final Terms") relating to the issue of the Notes for purpose of Article 8 of Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 and as it forms part of domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (EUWA) and must be read in conjunction with the Prospectus dated June 27, 2025, as supplemented by the supplement dated August 26, 2025 relating to the Programme (together, the "Prospectus").  Full information on Bank of Montreal and the offer of the Notes is only available on the basis of the combination of the Final Terms and the Prospectus.

DISCLAIMER - INTENDED ADDRESSEES

Please note that the information contained in the Prospectus and the Final Terms may be addressed to and/or targeted at persons who are residents of particular countries (specified in the Prospectus) only and is not intended for use and should not be relied upon by any person outside these countries and/or to whom the offer contained in the Final Terms is not addressed. Prior to relying on the information contained in the Final Terms you must ascertain from the Prospectus, as supplemented by these Final Terms, whether or not you are part of the intended addressees of the information contained therein.

The Notes have not been and will not be registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act") and are subject to U.S. tax law requirements.  Subject to certain exceptions, the Notes may not be offered, sold or delivered in the United States or to or for the account of a U.S. persons (as defined in Regulation S under the Securities Act).

data.title: Canada's Ivey PMI falls to three-month low in August
data.description:
Canadian economic activity barely expanded in August as employment declined and prices cooled, Ivey Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) data showed on Friday.

The seasonally adjusted index fell to 50.1 last month from 55.8 in July, posting its lowest level since May.

The Ivey PMI measures the month-to-month variation in economic activity as indicated by a panel of purchasing managers from across Canada. A reading above 50 indicates an increase in activity.

The gauge of employment dropped to an adjusted 46.0 from 51.2 in July, while the prices index was at 65.1, down from 68.5.

The unadjusted PMI fell to 50.0 from 54.6.

data.title: Lululemon slides to 5-1/2 year low after flagging dour holiday season
data.description:
** Shares of Lululemon Athletica tumble 16.5% to hit their lowest since March 2020 at $172.18

** Slashed annual sales, profit forecast on Thursday, signaling tepid holiday season

** At least 10 brokerages cut PTs on stock, two downgrade ratings

** "We are increasingly worried that LULU's noted high-value consumer attrition in "lounge" and "social" are increasingly turning to competitors Vuori and Alo Yoga," Stifel analysts say

** LULU expects 2025 net revenue to be in the range of $10.85 bln to $11 bln vs prior target of $11.15 bln to $11.30 bln

** Stock rated "hold" on average by 33 analysts; median PT is $220 - data compiled by LSEG

** Up to last close, stock is down ~46% YTD
